label: iter36-bots-can-surf
trigger: USER: learn the bots how to surf
feature: BotSurfControl (ai_main.c): airborne over a too-steep-to-stand face -> hold strafe INTO the bank (uphill of goal heading), level pitch, no jump; + surf ramps get item-bait midline pickups + 4 spread spawns
verified: bots build 445-646 ups on the ramps, 0-3 deaths (was 0 spd/66 telefrag-deaths from single spawn); dojo all 4 IN_DOSSIER no-reg
key_lesson: single info_player_deathmatch telefrags a bot field -> 0 speed/mass deaths; spread spawns essential for bot AND human surf testing
human_validation: bots dont cross finish yet (no completion); riding+speed works

label: iter37-finish-trigger-flyover
shipped: finish trigger 256-tall floor box -> tall full-corridor wall (lo_z-64..+768) reaching back over the last ramp tail (fx0-384); start trigger 128->256 tall. Fast surfers (bot AND human) at 600+ ups were flying OVER the short trigger, never registering finish/lap
bot_completion: still 0 finishes / 0 Race: lines -> race_finish_touch never logs -> raceStartTime likely not stamped for surfing bots (deeper, not chased now)
diagnostic: bots reach finish region (x5975 vs pad ~6000) at 392-646 ups, surf well, 5 timeouts/few deaths
regression: none: strafegen-only

label: iter38-bot-completion-diagnosed
investigation: instrumented race_start/finish_touch: 8 start-stamps (raceStartTime WORKS for bots) but 0 finish-touches even with trigger reaching back 1536 -> bots fall short of finish by >1 ramp (reach ~x5975, finish >x7511). Root cause = surf-control loses the line on later ramps, NOT trigger/raceStart
resolution: reverted band-aid (tx0 fx0-1536 -> -256), kept tall fly-over trigger from iter37, stripped debug
conclusion: full bot lap-completion = multi-ramp surf-control robustness = genuine multi-iteration AI work, not a quick fix
verified: qagame clean; dojo all 4 IN_DOSSIER
regression: none

label: iter39-bot-completion-concluded
attempts: (a) short 2-ramp surf line -> 0 finishes (surf 422ups, fails at transition not ramp-count); (b) transition pop-off (probe ahead, jump at ramp end) -> 0 finishes + LOWERED avgspd 422->346 (popping loses the line) -> reverted as net-negative
conclusion: bot surf LAP-COMPLETION defeats simple heuristics (short-map, tall-trigger, transition-pop all failed) = genuine multi-iteration surf-AI research; modest payoff (surf auto-test) since human playtest validates surf feel. MILESTONE bots-surf (445-646ups) stands.
kept: SurfLine ramps= param (harmless); clean iter36 surf control
verified: dojo all 4 IN_DOSSIER
regression: none

label: iter-bot-flow-combat
request: close gaps: flow in arches + combat at speed simultaneously
tried_1: air-strafe arcs (sticky) in BotApplyMoveset -> REGRESSED speed (ztrick 44%->26% time at 400-600); bots jittery aim flips the strafe, cancels air-accel; reverted
tried_2: combat-bhop (force hop+orbit when enemy near): arena frags 2->18, 94% midair (solves long-standing bots-dont-fight) BUT leaks onto movement dojos (bots target each other everywhere) -> SPEED/FLOW/ZTRICK PARTIAL/OUT even distance+speed gated; reverted
findings: 1) bot speed = bhop chain not air-strafe, dont override air move. 2) arena combat win is real but pit too small (448w) to flow AT speed + combat overrides bleed into movement. 3) flow-in-arches is a VIEW jitter problem (yaw rate), not a movement one
outcome: reverted to clean baseline (all 4 IN_DOSSIER); next steps are DESIGN: widen arena, smooth bot view yaw, or scope combat-bhop to arenas only

label: iter-flow-combat-round2
did_1_view_smooth: KEPT: BotChangeViewAngles travel turn-rate capped 360->160 deg/s + smooth model (was over-reaction snap). No-regression (all 4 IN_DOSSIER first-pass). But path heading-jitter still ~99deg/sample -> the erratic tracer is the bhop/air-strafe S-CURVE (speed technique), not the view, so visual impact is mild
did_2_arena_combat: REVERTED: combat-bhop orbit-dances (circle-strafe in place = slow, arena avgspd 141->97); wider pit let bots spread + stall; needs real combat-AI (bhop-rush approach), not a move override
key_finding: smooth arcs vs speed are in TENSION (zig-zag IS how bots go fast); combat-at-speed needs pathing work not overrides
state: clean baseline + mild view smoothing kept; both hard gaps remain, need deeper work or design calls

label: combat-at-speed-WIN
feature: bot_combatBhop (default ON; movement dojos force off): arena bots bhop-RUSH enemy (forward+jump=speed via rehop) + committed point-blank PEEL (g_botPass) to pass through not orbit; plus view turn-rate cap 360->160 kept
result: ARENA avgspd 141 to 245, peak 264 to 408, time-at-400plus 1pct to 28pct, flow 10 to 28pct, midair fragging (orbit attempt was 97)
verified: full dojo all 4 healthy; movement courses unaffected (cvar off there); SPEED 437/412/449 confirms earlier PARTIAL was maxspd noise
